entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements: Implement Visual Filters in Analytic List Page (ALP)
entry.content:
Thank you for this blog well explained but not working in my case.

What do you mean with "All these annotations need to be added as sub annotations of the attribute for which we are going to implement this visual filters" ?

entry.title: Re: Time Calculation Data Action - From To Period
entry.content:
Hi @Freddie 

Based on your scenario, you can get your expected result through the Advanced Formulas script below. 

MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "Amount"
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "202401" TO "202412"

//The formula checks if the current date falls within the locked period defined for the site. If it does, all data within this period is cleared.

IF (YEAR([d/Date]) * 12 + MONTH([d/Date])) - (YEAR([d/Site].[p/LockedFrom]) * 12 + MONTH([d/Site].[p/LockedFrom])) >= 0
	AND
   (YEAR([d/Date]) * 12 + MONTH([d/Date])) - (YEAR([d/Site].[p/LockedTo]) * 12 + MONTH([d/Site].[p/LockedTo])) <= 0 THEN

	DELETE()
ENDIF

To get the deleting Date scope based on the [d/Site].[p/LockedFrom] & [d/Site].[p/LockedTo] needs to compare with the Date dimension MEMBERSET scope(202401 ~ 202412) and Delete transaction data when including the Lock period of the Site dimension. 

In my testing model, all cells were filled with 100 and got the below result.

** Date dimension granularity is "Month".

** The data type of [p/LockedFrom] & [p/LockedTo] should be TEXT type.

I hope this is helpful to you, and if I have misunderstood anything, please feel free to reach out.

Moonjun

entry.title: KPI-Tile missing category after refresh
entry.content:
Hello,

I have created a KPI-tile for the Fiori-Launchpad that is based on a custom CDS-View.

I created an ODATA-Service for my view and also tested it in the SAP Gateway Client.
The data is shown as expected.

My problem is that the values in my KPI-tile are not working as I intended.
I have following values that I get from the CDS-View (these are the Values I want to show in my KPI-tile):


And when I log into the Fiori-Launchpad the tile looks how I wanted it to look:


But when I refresh the tile it looks like this:


When I refresh the whole website it shows the right values again.
But indepentend of the tile, the drilldown Report behind the KPI-tile always shows the wrong values:

My first thought was that I configured something wrong, but to me everything looks right:

I want it to look something like this, right now only one category is shown:

Has anyone experienced this, or can help me to solve it?

Lars

entry.title: Use of Glossary in Live System
entry.content:
This may be my ignorance.  It may also be that we don't have Web Assistant working correctly yet.

I thought that when we created the Glossary items, when you then open the Carousel, the glossary definitions would show against the words on our SAP Fiori screens?  If not can you access the Glossary from the Carousel?

Currently the Carousel is showing none of our training content, hence why I don't think it is fully activated.

If the user has to open the Glossary manually in a different screen from Fiori, then they will not be impressed.

entry.title: Re: CCM - Settlement date after contract end date
entry.content:
Hi Arindamsur, 

you have to allow 'Delta Settlement' for your contract type. Contract type -> Settlement Dates

Settlement Date Type:

  • 2: Delta Settlement: Delta settlements can be used to settle subsequent changes in the business volume base, for example, invoice cancellations or returns posted after a final settlement has been processed for a specific time period.

Regards,

Thorsten

entry.title: The Supply Chain Behind Oktoberfest: Tradition, Innovation and Sustainability
entry.content:
Oktoberfest isn’t just the largest folk festival of Bavarian culture and beer; it’s a masterclass in supply chain planning and logistics that provides an unforgettable experience for millions of visitors all around the world. With countless liters of beer flowing and thousands of meals served, this festival is a logistical marvel that showcases the power of complex planning and innovative solutions.

 

Over 7 million liters of beer were poured at Oktoberfest

At the heart of Oktoberfest is the legendary "Oktoberfest beer," brewed exclusively by six renowned breweries: Augustiner, Hacker-Pschorr, Hofbräu, Löwenbräu, Paulaner, and Spaten. These breweries don't just whip up beer overnight; they begin their production well in advance, following a traditional brewing method that has been honed over centuries.

This dedication is reflected in the extraordinary reputation of Munich's Oktoberfest, which adheres to strict quality standards, including the famous Reinheitsgebot (German Beer Purity Law). This law ensures that beer remains simple and pure by allowing only four ingredients: water, barley, hops, and yeast.

Once brewed, the beer starts on its journey to the festival grounds. Traditional wooden kegs and modern steel barrels are used for smaller-scale transportation and on-site storage, while tanker trucks transport large quantities of beer to the festival grounds.

But the real showstopper is the underground beer pipelines that transport beer directly from breweries to the festival tents. Paulaner's technical team says each beer tent is fitted with three 28,000-liter tanks connected by a 260-meter pipeline system, ensuring the beer remains fresh and cold. The automation system analyzes beer flow, temperature, and pressure, and sends hourly updates to the brewery staff via email or text regarding the beer usage from each tap station. This setup not only speeds up service but also reduces transportation costs and emissions.

 

An estimated 6 million visitors are expected to attend the festival

Oktoberfest poises to welcome 6 million visitors, who are expected to spend a staggering €400 million during the event.

To manage such a large number of visitors and ensure that supplies meet demand, organizers rely heavily on accurate forecasting. They utilize historical data and advanced analytics to predict consumption patterns throughout the festival duration. This helps in minimizing shortages and waste, ensuring that beer and food supplies are sufficient to cater to the expected crowds.

However, factors such as unexpected warm weather can significantly influence consumption rates. The weather at Oktoberfest has been as unpredictable as fall itself lately. Last year made everyone flock to the beer gardens, making seats in the festival tents hotter than a fresh pretzel out of the oven and ending up increasing beer consumption. This meant organizers had to quickly adjust their supply logistics to make sure they didn’t run out of stock.

Also, real-time digital systems play a crucial role in inventory management at the festival. Organizers can monitor beer consumption across various tents to manage stock levels effectively and ensure timely refills. This technology not only enhances operational efficiency but cuts down the wait times for drinks, allowing everyone to spend more time enjoying the event rather than waiting in line.

 

500k whole roast chickens and 60k sausages are consumed

While the beer takes center stage during the 16-day festival, the food is equally iconic, as well as the numbers. Visitors chow down on:

  • 124 oxen
  • 29 calves
  • 509,420 roast chickens
  • 122,658 pairs of pork sausages
  • 80,259 knuckles of pork and many other delicious Bavarian food.

Navigating economic pressures, regulatory requirements, and logistical hurdles is no small task for suppliers who must coordinate with local farms and distributors to deliver fresh ingredients right on time. Ensuring compliance with food safety regulations adds another layer of complexity to this already intricate operation.

 

Consumers are prompting suppliers to rethink their sourcing and waste management strategies

In recent years, Oktoberfest has prioritized sustainability within its supply chain, positioning itself as one of the most eco-friendly large-scale events worldwide. By sourcing only Munich-based breweries, the festival already minimizes transportation distances and the resulting emissions.

Many of these breweries utilize environmentally friendly packaging, including returnable bottles or kegs that can be reused up to 50 times. Additionally, soft drinks are sold solely in returnable bottles with a deposit system, while canned beverages are prohibited, significantly reducing waste generation.

Since 2000, Oktoberfest has powered all public areas with renewable energy sources. The festival utilizes green electricity and natural gas which helps offset approximately 1,000 tonnes of CO2 emissions annually.

The supply chain behind Oktoberfest is a remarkable blend of tradition, innovation, and sustainability. This festival not only highlights Bavarian traditions but also serves as a model for how large-scale events can operate sustainably, ensuring that future generations can continue to enjoy this iconic celebration. Prost to a successful Oktoberfest and greener supply chains of the future!

To discover essential strategies for developing risk-resilient and sustainable supply chains, download the Oxford Economics Report: “5 Supply Chain Strategies to Achieve Resiliency and Avoid Risk.”

entry.title: Fill Port details in E-waybill XML for Export scenario
entry.content:
How to send the Port details in the E-Waybill XML for export scenario?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Fill Port details in E-waybill XML for Export scenario
entry.content:
The Export Process can be done as follows:

  1. Maintain Bill-To Party as a Foreign Customer.
  2. Do not Maintain any Ship-To Party if the receiving party is the same foreign customer. If Ship-To is maintained, ensure that it is the Foreign Customer.
  3. Use a Z Partner Function for the Port ID related address to be filled in the Ship-To address.

Note: Do not confuse with the Port ID and Ship-To.
Ship-To should be Foreign Customer and the Port Address will be filled using the Z-Partner Function. Refer E14 of the TSG 3415015 - GST IN: India Eway bill automatic solution: Troubleshooting guide and FAQ

Process to fill Port Address: (Do not use the standard Partner Functions, use Z-Partner Functions that will created in Step 2)

  1. Create each port as customer
  2. Create a new partner function and assign this partner function at the time sales order or delivery with port customer master(Creating partner explained in KBA 2921673)
    2921673 - GST IN: How to capture Export Port details for generating eInvoice & eWay bill
  3. Maintain the partner function which is created in step 2 in config view  SSCUI - 102705.(This step is explained in implementation guide from the note 2631687 - SAP solution for e-Way Bill India)

entry.title: SF Stories – Dynamic Date Settings - Keep last saved values
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We've recently observed that newly created or saved Optimised Design Report Stories were not displaying the expected dynamic date. For example, a report intended to show today’s date (e.g., 25 Sep) was instead showing the date when the report was last saved (e.g., Sep 4). Please see the attached screenshot for reference.

This issue occurs because the ‘Keep last saved values for dynamic variables’ box was automatically checked. You can find this setting by navigating to: Tools > Edit Prompts > Data Model > Settings. To resolve the issue, the box needs to be unchecked and the report needs to be saved.

We came across an SAP SAC Help page about this default setting - Set Story Variables (Optimized), but we haven’t found any SuccessFactors updates regarding its implementation as a default option in the embedded version of Stories. Classic View Stories reports are not impacted by this change.

We would like to understand this change and its functionality better. Our questions are:

  1. Is there a blog or roadmap reference with more information about this change?
  2. Is there a way to disable this default setting that does not require revisiting all newly created or modified optimised Story reports?

Thank you for your help.

Best regards,
Zaklin

entry.title: Re: Identity Federation in IAS
entry.content:
Hi

Not sure what you want to achieve in general, I guess you just want to pass the users and groups from EntraID to the app as per your configuration. The note "Users and groups from Azure are provisioned to IAS and granted access in BTP" was not clear, I guess you're not actually provisioning the users and creating them in IAS using IPS or SCIM and you just want to use IAS as a proxy.

I'd suggest to check using SAML Tracer tool (it's available for Mozilla, Chrome, etc). You'll be able to understand the attributes flow from EntraID/IAS/Application

entry.title: Re: Query for Prodution order nº, Receipt from Production...
entry.content:
By system behavior it is not possible to name the lables on your own.

It is the date in the production order

And it is start and end

regards Lothar

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - Remote Table dynamic filter pushdown
entry.content:
Hi @chrisesco ,

i use a S/4 HANA on-Premise connection as well.
Regarding HANA: The pushdown capabilites when wokring with a direct HANA (database) connection are way better than working with S4 (application). Thats why here you can also push down joins etc.

Regarding RepFlow: There could be a wide number of errors. You might check SLG1 transaction in the S4 or maybe the Cloud connector needs some RFC configuration.

entry.title: Re: Usage decision with HUM and WM active: Part-II
entry.content:
Hi, Check in VL06O that delivery in this case was created with position type HSTP

BR

entry.title: Re: Tasks not displaying in process quality tasks app and manage tasks app
entry.content: Thanks Jonathan. Got the idea. But instead of answering you have replied. Since I could not accept the answer.

entry.title: Re: Recommended implementation of a dependent branch abro...
entry.content:
Hello PPA1,

 

The SAP solution depends on the company structure that you have for the new branch. If the new branch is a registered legal entity in France then having a separate company code is a better option. It will make a lot of things easy for business. However, if the branch is an extension of the German entity and only has VAT liability in France then explore the standard SAP function of the plant abroad. You can separate the France tax codes within the German tax procedure with different series of numbers.

 

Thanks

Avnish

entry.title: Re: RAP Create button still enabled after disabling the feature
entry.content: I have the same issue. delete its working as expected. But for create get_global_features is not getting called...

entry.title: Update due date of vendor BoE
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Is it possible to update the due date before processing a Returned Bill of Exchange Payable?

Steps:

  1. Enter a vendor invoice with the Bill of Exchange (BoE) payment method.
  2. Process the automatic payment for the invoice, indicating a due date for the bill of exchange (BoE).
  3. Use the "Returned Bill of Exchange Payable" app to post the payment once the bill of exchange payable's due date is reached, and your bank has paid the bill.

Our question is: Can we update/change the due date of the bill of exchange (BoE) between steps 2 and 3? Is this possible?

Regards,

entry.title: Business process automation form failure
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing this issue in Business process automation form. After the workflow is getting approved by a user, the instance goes into the following error:  Failed to build headers. The destination tried to provide authorization tokens but failed in all cases. This is most likely due to misconfiguration. Original error messages: Retrieval of OAuthToken failed due to: Unable to fetch refresh token from the specified token service URL. Response was Token header claim [kid] references unknown signing key :

In-Person

entry.title: MC9A not possible for info structure with Material/Productgroup. what to do?
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to setup SOP for the first time with an example for a single end product. I created an info structure S999 as a copy of S076 and got stuck with the step Generate Master Data and generate proportional factors MC9A. as per https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/sales-operation-planning/qaq-p/8155245. 

When I enter the info structure S999 with Version 000 and execute i get the message MA350 - Info structure is not planned consistently. MY IS S999 same as S076 has the plannting type I - stage planning. 

1. What can I do now?

2. How can I set up SOP for a single material in an easy way to have the sales figures for a material per plant?

entry.title: Not able to Fetch Purchase Info Record Automatically in STO PO for India Localization
entry.content:
Hello SAP Gurus,

I am facing an issue in STO Indian scenario, where in i have maintained the Purchase info Record against the Supplying Plant (Vendor) with the tax code but while creating the STO PO, the system which is supposed to Fetch the Purchase Info Record automatically, is not fetching it and i have to enter Purchase info record number manually every time i create a STO to trigger tax amount from the tax code maintained in PIR, unlike in THE Standard PO where the system fetches it automatically.

Kindly suggest if there is any standard solution for this issue.

#STOIndia #PurchaseInfoRecord 

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Create CAP CDS entity via synonyms
entry.content: Thanks Mio.Appreciate your help.

entry.title: Sample Analysis + SIGNAL Query for double execution events in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content:
Intro

This blog is a bit complementary to material in SAP Learning material related to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. The data set used for this demonstration is the same one provided in the course Analyzing Business Processes with SAP Signavio Solutions

 

Objective

In this blig I will focus quite specifically on analyzing Events that failed ❗to execute at 1st attempt  in the process, thus a second attempt took place for successful execution, via the creation of a widget that can be used for further action and deeper process execution analysis.

Since I am using simple data sets, in order for the audience to relate to the content to the learning material in the course above listed, I will stay high level and will not go into cost analysis in this blog. The storyboard for this blog does create awareness however, on the need to analyse the cost associated with events/tasks that are failing to be completed in alignment with defined cycle times.

This blog does not focus on process set up and data loads.

 

Process Discovery

An investigation in Process Intelligence is created and the inital Process Discovery widget becomes available. 

Taking a first look at it with "Low Path Granularity" allows for a quick assessment of the general flow and the potential variant proliferation. The case count view provides the following information.

The above information rapidly provides info that the total number of case_ids in the data set to be analysed is 877, confirmed by the process start with 877 'Receive Customer Order'  at the START and at the END by 805 'Receive Delivery Confirmation' + 72 'Order Canceled' 

It is also noticeable that event "Ship Goods Standard" is part of 445 case_ids.

Let us take a look at the same diagram but with event Event Count View.

The event count view now allows for better understanding on the execution of the process and already it is clear that some events for 'Ship Goods Standard' and 'Ship Goods Express' have failed at first attempt and had to be executed twice.

Let us focus on 'Ship Goods Standard' . We can determine that 106 occurrences out of the 551 were executed twice, which is in alignment with the unique case_id / event information of 445 case_ids from the Case Count View

We can do the math simply here at high level: thus for 339 case_ids 'Ship Goods Standard' was executed successfully at first try ✔️, and for 106 case_ids 'Ship Goods Standard' was executed twice ⚠️for the event (process task) to be executed to completion.

But as we all know, there are many ways to slice and dice the process executions 😅  and as part of the Investigations different widgets are needed to better understand where the issues are 🤔

Process Variant Explorer Widget

Creating a Chapter called: High Level Analysis and adding widgets there is a recommended first step to identify insights at a higher level, which later one can be looked into by other process stakeholders (i.e Business Analysts, Process SMEs) after formal actions are assigned.

Taking a look at the Variant Explorer widget with No Filters on Attributes proves information that there are 2 Variants where the above occurrance behavioir is taking place (variant 5 and 😎

Process Conformance Widget

Let us know take a look at the Process Conformance widget without filters to analyse the Hot Spots and confirm the inital observations

(Note: the AS-IS Diagram was modelled also for another blog, related to conformance, thus I am not pointing out conformance in this blog)

The Hot Spot view in this widget, after analysing all variants, provides the information that Variant 5 and 8 are the ones where 'Ship Goods Standard' is executed twice:  72 occurences for variant 5 and 34 for variant 8.

With the above information we are starting to go deeper into the analysis and have better ideas of what further investigation actions will be needed for deeper analysis, collaboration with other stakeholders and improvement.

Based on this observations, an Insight can be created to be assigned and shared with others (i.e. Business Analysts)

Creating Actions

In this blog' storyboard, I am taking the role of Lead Process Owner, and I am starting to define what actions are important in relation to tackling issues impacting Value Drivers.

With the information above in this Investigation, I can now create a special query in Signal that will support me in engaging other stakeholders and request to deep dive in the investigation, and create their own Chapters with further Widgets. 

let us assument that there are different Business Analysts focusing on the Orders in different locations. "City" is an attribute in the log for this data set. 

So I will create an additional table that creates awarness on how the ocurrences of 'Ship Goods Standard' are distributed across all cities.

 

In this action, I am using SIGNAL to create a brand new table that will be shared with all Business Analysts, for each to focus on situation per City, and how data changes while the investigation is open. 

In this example, the intial action creates awareness on the Action related to 'Ship Goods Standard' analysis and specific tasks can be created for each stakeholder with specific instructions. For example, that each one continues deeper level investigations per location in specific chapters withing an Investigation.

Here we are using "CITY" as example, but lets say that we are analysing similar issues in double execution events in Supply Planning process acroess different Plants. A Process Owner could determine to breakdown the analysis in Chapters per "PLANT". 

 

Summary

Investigations in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence provide the Process Owners, Business Analysts and and other involved stakeholders with numerous ways to zoom into different issues via the use of standard widgets but also via the creation of additional views or adjustments to standard widgets when using SIGNAL queries.

Each organization might decide on specific (WoWs) Ways of Working using investigations in Process Intelligence; one important aspect is to use the Investigation / Chapter /  Action feature in way that breaks down the analysis per order of impact to business and promoting further deeper analysis depending on the complexity of the process execution issue to be tackled. 

About the Author

JD Wong-Loera is a Stockholm/Toronto based Project Manager, Business Architect and Process Management consultant who enjoys supporting others in understanding the Businesss Architecture and Business Process Management Capabilities. In his free time he enjoys camping, reading and boxing.

Check out my other blogs at: About JD_WongLoera - SAP Community

entry.title: Dispatcher Reminder (BusinessProcessSteps) in Query and Business rules
entry.content:

I would like to create a business rule that refers to a dispatcher reminder (BusinessProcessSteps) in SAP Field Service Management #. Unfortunately, I have not been able to query this accordingly. I have not been able to do it using "IN" as in the "responsibles" field, for example. I have also not found any documentation for the "List" type. Has anyone in the community ever had this happen

BR

Matthias

entry.title: Re: User-defined fields order
entry.content:
Hi

open the object and the UDF-panel.

Go through Tools -> Customisation Tools - Settings

Change the order

regards Lothar

entry.title: Help! Why Don’t I Get a Notification for My Custom Situation?
entry.content:
You’ve configured your own situation use case using the Extended Framework for Situation Handling. Now you’re waiting for a notification but nothing happens. What could have gone wrong?

Let me ask you a few questions to check whether your configuration is correct.

  • Has a situation occurred?
  • How have the conditions been set up?
  • Are all situation triggers covered?
  • Is recipient determination working?
  • Have email notifications been set up correctly?

These questions can support you as an SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP S/4HANA customer.

 

Has a situation occurred?

This may seem trivial but depending on the conditions that have been defined for the situation type, a situation instance may not have been created. An easy way to find out is for the user is to check the My Situations – Extended app to see if there’s an instance that they should have been notified about. If the instance is not displayed in the app, continue checking the conditions. If there’s an instance but no notification was sent, check the notifications / life cycle settings.

List of situaitons in My Situations – Extended app

Or, as a key user, you can display the current situation instances for a situation type by choosing Manage Instances in the header of the Manage Situation Types – Extended app.

Manage Instances in Manage Situation Types - Extended app

In the list of situation instances you can see if a notification was sent and when. Depending on the configuration, a notification can be sent only when a situation occurs for the first time or each time the situation is updated. If you don’t see an entry here, no notification was sent.

List of situation instances in Manage Situation Types - Extended app

There’s also the Monitor Situations – Extended app for key users that shows all created situation instances and all activities for an instance. You need to have situation monitoring enabled for the situation type in the Situation Instances section.

Enable the monitoring in Manage Situations - Extended app

List of situation instances in the Monitor Situations - Extended app

If no situation instance is displayed in the Manage Situation Types – Extended app and in the Monitor Situations – Extended app, this means that the users did not receive a notification because there no situation instance was created.  

If you expected a situation based on the conditions that you have defined, you may find clues in the following section on conditions.

If a situation instance is displayed but no notification was sent, check the notifications / life cycle setting.

How have the conditions been set up?

In the Conditions section of the Manage Situation Types – Extended app, let’s look at the settings for Notifications / Life Cycle, the Order of the conditions, and the Filters.

Notifications / life cycle settings

Each condition can have a different Notifications/Life Cycle setting. Make sure you’ve assigned the appropriate ones.

Notification setting

You can select from three standard options:

  1. The notification is sent to all recipients when a situation is created. When the situation is updated, only the assigned processors are notified.
  2. No one receives a notification after a situation is closed.
  3. All recipients receive a notification after a closed situation has been reopened (this option is available only for object-based situations with the instance closing behavior Close & Keep)

If you’ve defined your own Notification/Life Cycle behavior, I recommend double-checking the life cycle transitions. Make sure you have considered all relevant transitions from creation to closure. And check the notification settings for each transition.

Consider the processing order of conditions

If you’ve set up multiple conditions, the order of those conditions is important. The order defines the sequence in which the conditions are executed. It’s important to understand that the processing of the conditions stops when the first condition in the processing order is met. Conditions lower in the sequence are ignored. For this reason, you need to ensure that the more specific conditions, for which you want to have notifications, are being processed before the more generic conditions. Pay special attention to conditions that do not send notifications and those that close instances. If they are too high up in the sequence other conditions may always be ignored.

Processing order of conditions

Condition filters

Effective filters need to be of the same data format as the values in the CDS fields, such as strings or integers. You can find the reference to your object’s CDS views in the Manage Situation Objects app.

Sometimes the conditions and their filter values have been set up in such a way that no instances are created. Check whether one of the following reasons has prevented the creation of a situation instance:

  • The condition attribute filters are too constrained for your use case.
  • The attribute filter values overlap.
  • The attribute filter values are contradictory.

The condition and filter settings depend on your use case and the situation template on which your situation type is based. If you use multiple conditions, you can easily compare filters using the Compare Filters function.

Compare condition filters

Compare condition filters used

You can find some examples of using multiple conditions with filters in the blog posts Custom Situation Cases: Configure an Escalation Case and Custom Situation Cases: Configure a Complex Case.

 

Are all situation triggers covered?

Complex use cases often require multiple situation triggers. Check whether you have configured all possible triggers. And ensure that all triggers are enabled. There are two kinds of triggers: batch-based and event-based triggers.

Batch-based triggers periodically check the data you have defined in the condition filters.

  • The triggering object can be the anchor object or the trigger object.
  • The behavior of the trigger object is always Add/Update because the object still exists.

Use the Trigger Batch function to test immediately whether the situation trigger works as expected. Also check batch scheduling to make sure it’s configured correctly.

Batch-based situation triggers

Event-based triggers are based on an object’s status update. You can use multiple event-based triggers where you need to consider three aspects:

  • The triggering object: Multiple objects may impact a situation.
  • The trigger events such as create, update, and remove: Multiple events may impact a situation for a specific object.
  • The behavior of the trigger object can be Add/Update, Remove (Trigger data available), or Remove (Trigger data not available)

Event-based situation triggers

And these three aspects need to go hand in hand.

Situation objects that are created always have the trigger object behavior Add/Update because the object is added and exists.

The events that change an object can be manifold, for example, updated, confirmed, rejected, in progress, for approval, completed, released, posted, published, started, activated, blocked, unlocked, and many more. If the object still exists after the change, the trigger object behavior is Add/Update. If the object is flagged as removed or deleted, the trigger object behavior is Remove (Trigger data available). 

Objects that are deleted always have the trigger object behavior Remove (trigger data not available). This means the object is physically gone along with all its data. No more references are possible.

You can find an example for multiple triggers in the blog post Custom Situation Cases: Configure a Complex Case.

Should a situation trigger be missing in the situation type, you have the following options in the test system:

  1. Add a situation trigger from the value help in the situation type.
  2. If no appropriate situation trigger is available for the situation type, you can add a situation trigger with the suitable event to your situation scenario (Manage Situation Scenarios app). To do this you need to have the extensibility specialist role. Then proceed with (1).  Optionally, you can add the situation trigger to the related template so that it’s automatically available for all situation types created from the situation template.
  3. If no matching situation trigger is available in the situation scenario, you can enhance or add a situation object with the missing situation trigger (Manage Situation Objects app). Again, you need the extensibility specialist role. Then proceed with (2) and (1).

Afterwards, transport your situation type, situation scenario, and situation object to the productive system.

 

Is recipient determination working?

Situation Handling uses Responsibility Management to determine the users that are responsible for a situation. In the My Situations – Extended app, users see the created situations, and, depending on the notification settings, receive notifications.

When you create a situation type in the Recipients section, there are two basic options for defining these users:

  • Responsibility by Teams, which determines the users with the help of a team category.
  • Responsibility Rules, which determines the users with a BAdI business logic.

Situation recipients

Responsibility by Teams

To determine the right users by teams, a team category needs to be in place. The team category links Situation Handling and Responsibility Management. For a team category, at least one team needs to be enabled so that the right users can be detected. You enable teams in the Manage Teams and Responsibilities app. Also check whether all enabled teams have team members assigned to them and include the users who have to be informed about the situation.

Situation recipients by teams

You can add team members by selecting users from the value help. If you can’t find the user in the value help, check whether this user is registered as a business partner. If the business partner registration is missing, contact your system administrator to have your users registered.

If you use filters for responsibility definition or member functions in your situation type, make sure they are appropriate and don’t exclude your users. For detailed information about these filters, see the blog post Using Responsibility Teams in Situation Handling.

Responsibility Rules

Responsibility rules use the logic of a BAdI to determine the right users. If the identification of users isn’t working as expected, the logic of the BAdI can be the reason why your users were not found and therefore did not get any notifications.

Situation recipients by rules

If your situation type uses a standard responsibility rule, please open a ticket on component CA-GTF-RM to have the rule checked.

For your custom implementation, you can check the BAdI implementation logic yourself. (You’ll need the SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC role to do that). Then proceed as follows:

  • In the Manage Responsibility Context app, on the Extended tab, search for the parent responsibility context to find your extended responsibility context.
  • Open the extended responsibility context to display the details. Here, you can see the responsibility rule ID associated with the responsibility rule that is displayed in the situation type.
  • In the Custom Logic app, you need to filter the list for the responsibility rule business context. Then, search for the static filter value that is identical to the responsibility rule ID. In the details, you can then check the BAdI implementation logic.

For detailed information about custom responsibility rules, you can refer to the blog post Responsibility Management: Create Your Rules. The blog post Using Responsibility Rules in Situation Handling illustrates how responsibility rules and Situation Handling work hand in hand.

 

Have email notifications been set up correctly?

In addition to the SAP Fiori launchpad notifications, users can opt to receive email notifications for each situation type in their SAP Fiori launchpad settings. For details, see Enable Email Notifications for End Users.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 email notifications are enabled automatically. In older releases users need to enable email notifications in their SAP Fiori launchpad settings. To be able to activate this option, users need to have received an SAP Fiori launchpad notification before they can see and select the situation type in the launchpad settings.

Notification settings

Users may also want to check the launchpad settings to see whether the email address for their user account is correct:

Email settings

If all the settings are correct and you're still not getting any emails, you may want to check whether the email domain is on the list of allowed domains. The list of sender domains is maintained in Output Management. You can find more information in the chapter Defining an Email Sender Domain in the Setup Guide for SAP Best Practices for Output Management.

For specific questions or issues with the notification framework, please open a ticket on component OPU-GW-NOT.

 

Additional prerequisites for SAP S/4HANA

As an SAP S/4HANA customer, you may need to check some additional settings for the Notification Channel Framework which are automatically in place for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers. You can also refer to the blog post Enable Email Notification in Notification Channel Framework. If you want to enable email notifications per default, you can implement the SAP Note 3417113.

To be on the safe side, you may also want to check whether you have made the required technical configuration settings.

  • Set up technical jobs
  • Activate OData services for apps
  • Define authorization for apps (optional)
  • Activate OData services for APIs (optional)
  • Define authorizations for APIs (optional)

 

Did this help you?

If not → just open a ticket on our component CA-SIT-ENG and we’ll take care of your problem. In the ticket, please let us know the situation scenario and the situation template your situation type is based on. Please attach screenshots of your situation type’s Conditions and Recipients sections. Or tell us which of the steps above you’ve already tried.

 

For more information, see

SAP Help Portal for

  • Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA.

SAP Community

  • Intelligent Situation Handling

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation – SMTP Mail Destination using Microsoft 365 email provider
entry.content: Very comprehensive tutorial, thank you @KarthikeyanMurthy!

entry.title: Re: Asset transfer (value adjustment)
entry.content: Dear Aninoo_Petro, You are correct in describing that Land should not be depreciated. Since the original asset (sending asset) has accumulated dep hence it has posted the dep into adjustment. The only way to fix is to use t code ABZU using transaction type 700 and transfer the accumulated value back to APC. Thanks Avnish

entry.title: Accounting for Inventory In-Transit (Plant to Plant transfer)
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We have encountered an issue with accounting performed by SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud not aligned with the expected accounting entry by the business - Key details as shown below.
Can you please review and share if we can achieve the expected accounting entry in SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud (as its supported by SAP SAP On-premises).

Business Scenario:
Plant A transfers Material to Plant B ( Both Plant A and B are in the same company code)

Standard Public Cloud setup:
Accounting entry at the time of Goods Issue from A:
Dr. Stock Account in Plant B
Cr. Stock Account in Plant A

What is the opportunity/problem the request will address?

No Accounting entry is generated at the time of Goods receipt in Plant B.
The “Display Stock in Transit” report is available to view the inventory status for materials that have been issued but received.

Impact:
SAP S4 HC has since indicated that the system does not support transactions for inventory in-transit between inventory locations within a single company code. It only accommodates inventory in-transit for intercompany activity. We are concerned that the lack of inventory in-transit functionality for movements between factories and 3PL’s as well as between plants will create tracking and reconciliation issues.

What is the expected benefit?

On-Premise solution help - Valuated Stock in Transit | SAP Help Portal

Requirement from Business:

Accounting entry at the time of Goods Issue from Plant A:

Dr. Stock in Transit a/c

Cr. Stock Account in Plant A

 

Accounting entry at the time of Goods Receipt in Plant B:

Dr. Stock Account in Plant B

Cr. Stock in Transit a/c

 

Regards

entry.title: Issues interacting with PDF Documents inside of SAP GUI
entry.content:
We have an issue inside of SAP GUI (800 Patch 7, although issue appears in 760 and 770 where the buttons that appear when viewing a PDF Print Preview are not able to be clicked on -

Open ME23N and select a Purchase Order

Click Print Preview then PDF Preview

The PDF is correctly displayed, however the buttons to Save, Print or Zoom cannot be clicked on.

We have tried switching from Edge to Internet Explorer/Adobe HTML control, moving SAP versions, themes ect. but are still unable to interact with PDF documents,

entry.title: Re: How to view MVC in NWDS 7.5 for SAP EP(Portal) from SLD
entry.content:
Hello,

 

You have to import the SLD in the NWDS (there will be a icon on top menu to do this)

Once imported, you have to open the Development Infrastructure perspective and create the Project locally in your NWDS and then you can deploy it by right click on Project

entry.title: Requirement to Data model in XSC envrionment but combine XSA Models
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

I have XSC and XSA both envrionment and now i need to create new data model in XSC envrionment where i have to use XSA calculation views.

I will create a new XSC calculation view by combine few views from XSC and Few views from XSA and provide the final view to reporting.

Please advise.

Is it possible to access XSA Calviews in XSC classic environment? how?

 

Regards

Rajesh Ramakrishnan

entry.title: mC93 - no material type for product groups exist in table T134
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to run MC93 after haveing created an info structure S976 as a copy of S076, maintained MC7F, MC8C (created the planungs type as a copy of the standard SOPKAPAM) and after having done MC61 I tried to run MC93, enered a mat nr and the plant, and got the error message MA151 no material type for product groups exist in table T134.

I created a mat type PROD and then also a Zxxx in OMS2, both with 1 for product group type but in T134 the field KZGRP is empty. is this the reason why I face the issue? what shall I do now?

 

best regards,

WalterMe

entry.title: Re: Asset transfer (value adjustment)
entry.content: Thanks avnish_goyal1984 for your help. But i faced something in this tc ABZU which is when i simulate the run of transaction ABZU what will happen is DR. Asset (which perfect) CR.revenue (here the credit side will impact into revenue so i didn’t want to impact revenue it is shall impact CR.expense how i can solve this ?

entry.title: Re: 🔴 Automate your SAP Cloud Integration governance with CPILint
entry.content:
Hi Morten,

Thanks for the New lint version very helpful tool!

I have a question / suggestion : do we have rules regarding performance checks ?

Thanks & Regards

entry.title: Has anyone successfully used SAP BTP DMS for storage of SARA Data Archive?
entry.content:
We have created a CMIS repository and configured in OAC0/SM59/OA2C_CONFIG. Connectivity all appears successful.

When we run an archiving session to the new ArchiveLink/CMIS Repository, the file 'write' phase completes, but the 'store' session returns a BA111 error as it can't find the document id in the repository. We do see (via CMIS workbench) a /Knowledge Provider/Archivelink/<document id> folder created in the repository, but the 'data' object beneath this is of 0 length.

I think therefore that connectivity is OK as folders are created, it's just that the data is not copied successfully from the archive file to the repository. 

Has anyone else had this type of problem? The symptoms match many previous forum entries where the S BA111 message is followed by E BA194 in the job log, but I suspect the root cause is probably quite different here.

Is there anywhere within BTP where any error logs from the Integration service can be viewed?

entry.title: Re: Receivables Collection Management
entry.content:
Hello @thomas_meder 

The link to the Collection Case data source download above doesn't seem to be working, are you able to provide me with a new link please?

Many thanks!

Podtris

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data: BTP Free Tier
entry.content:
Hello Hozumi,

I am trying to consume the DQM but when I am trying to add the entitlement then the Data service UI is not available there only, I can add the data quality services.

entry.title: Re: Asset transfer (value adjustment)
entry.content: Hi Aninoo_Pedro, This is coming from account determination setting that you have in AO90.

entry.title: Re: M-series Apple Chip MacBooks and Abap Platform Trial containers using Docker and Podman
entry.content:
Hi @TomHoepping ,

glad to hear you got the 1909 image working using Docker Desktop and/or Orbstack.

For me, the constraints on a solution that would count as "Better than QEMU-enabled full VM emulation" are:

- must work with latest version of macOS

- must work with latest version of Rosetta 2

- must work with Abap Cloud Developer Trial image (currently the 2022 tagged release)

For macOS Sequoia, latest Rosetta 2, and latest Abap CDT, as you mention this isn't working (tested also myself last week). Of those 3 constraints, the only one I'd be willing to break for POC purposes is the one about the version of Rosetta 2 - so if you put a link to that blog-post where someone has downgraded Rosetta 2, then I can also take a look at it. Though if you want to be the first one to test that workaround, then that's also ok, in that case looking forward to hear how your test goes...

entry.title: Re: Asset transfer (value adjustment)
entry.content: In AO90, you have GL account maintained in the “Revenue from write-up on ord.deprec.”. You can either change this setting to the account you want to use. We usually maintain a dep expense account in this setting as you are reversing the dep. Moreover, if we need to transfer this amount to some other account, it is easy to post a journal with dep expense account.

entry.title: Re: selecting a cell in sac table
entry.content: For to filter de dimension in a detailed table clicking in a header table you can try “Linked Analysis”. You set it in the header table and you have to select “Filter on datapoint selection”

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
@David_Chaviano 
I'm Mohamed, and I'm really excited to join the SAP Community Beginner Corner. I work for a company that helps businesses in the Middle East get the most out of SAP solutions. Our goal is to empower companies to use SAP to their full potential.

I'm eager to learn as much as I can from this community and share my experiences as I grow in my SAP journey. Feel free to reach out if you have any questions or want to chat. Let's get started!"

entry.title: Re: Performance Rating and Comments Upload via Integration Centre in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Dear @Karthi_Viswa  @JohannD 

Thanks for sharing this blog and knowledge.

One doubt on the functionality, It's still not clear for me if we need to load comments on behalf of a manager if we can do this with a technical api user and comments be saved on the manager name or not. E.g: our technical user is api_sf user id and manager id is  manager1.

Our requirement is to save comments in competencies on behalf of the  manager and we have done some checks but we havent succeeded. Therefore, we have this doubt, if even using an api user "api_sf" we can operate on behalf manager1

 (Prerequisite Step 2)

  • formcontentid (system generates)
  • formdataid (system generates)
  • itemid (system generates)
  • ratingType (system generates)
  • sectionIndex (system generates)
  • userId - USERID
  • ratingkey - (system generates)
  • rating (user)
  • commentkey - (system generates)
  • comment (user)

I have requirement to filter Top 5 or Bot 5 contributors on a Measure but I want it to be driven through the Input field Dropdown with two values TOP 5 and BOT 5. when I select the values the Table_1 and chart_1 should have a particular Measure restricted to top 5 values or Bottom 5 values.

I have tried below code but it is not working as expected. I think it is possible to do it via Scripting changing filters of table and chart . Can someone help me on below code issue.

Thanks & Regards

Mohammed

entry.title: Re: Everything you need to know about: SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub
entry.content:
Hi,

We are migrating from JDM to JPB and, right after that, to TIH. Once we migrate to JBP, is it necessary to enable COC before migrating to TIH?

entry.title: Re: Not able to Fetch Purchase Info Record Automatically in STO PO for India Localization
entry.content: Hi Sunil, Are you creating the STO with reference to PIR that you have created? Unless you use PIR to create PO, the system won't fetch the information from PIR. Thanks Avnish

entry.title: Re: Use of Glossary in Live System
entry.content: SAP Companion does not support the Glossary. There has been rumors of it coming, but I haven't seen it on the Roadmap which means 'no time soon'... Even then, it will only link text in Bubbles; it's not going to magically turn text on the application screen into glossary links. The best you can do at the moment is use hotspots on screen elements, and provide definitions in the bubble texts. You could include your Glossary in the Trainer and then provide a link to the Glossary page from the Carousel (or co-opt the Community button if you don't use that for anything else...) but other than that, the functionality you are looking for does not exist.

entry.title: Automatic account assignment category in purchase requisition based on material type
entry.content:
Is it possibile to fill the account assignment category automatic based on material type in a purchase requisition? Our client has some materials that have a customized material type ''non value'', so account assignment category needs to be free of charge while default is normally on cost center. 

The requisitions are made by people who not always know if material is free of charge, so requirement is to fill the account assignment type automatically when material with material type 'non value' is entered. 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: M-series Apple Chip MacBooks and Abap Platform Trial containers using Docker and Podman
entry.content:
Hi @dylan-drummond 

I see it the same way. 1909 is not a real option for practical use, just a test for comparison and learning. 
Same for the rosetta downgrade, probably not an option for daily usage. Not sure if a down-port has to be done then with every minor patch/update. Interesting links are the following. I guess the tutorial will also work in the opposite way. 
https://www.reddit.com/r/macgaming/comments/1dd8jvs/tutorial_backport_rosetta_2_runtime_with_avx2/
https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=25547896

entry.title: G/L account 2298841 where the balance of $2,811.63 still remains open
entry.content:
Dear Friends,

This is related to the G/L account 2298841 where the balance of $2,811.63 still remains open as of today . However, we have released all VFX3 and VF04 for FY2024. We are in ECC 6.0

Please help to clear the balance of $2,811.63 for FY2024.

Regards
Sridhar

entry.title: 415 unsupported Mediatype issue in CAP
entry.content:
UI CODE :

 

 

_genericPostUploadFile: function (url, fileData, token, that, successCallback, errorCallback) {
					const formData = new FormData();
					formData.append('file', fileData);
					jQuery.ajax({
						type: "POST",
						url: url,
						processData: false,
                        contentType: false,
						data: formData,
						beforeSend: function (xhr) {
							xhr.setRequestHeader("X-CSRF-Token", token);
						},
						success: function (result) {
							if (successCallback) {
								successCallback(that, result);
							}

						},
						error: function (e) {
							if (errorCallback) {
								errorCallback(that, e);
							}
						}
					});
			

			},

 

 
 
CAP CODE :

 

    action uploadLogFilePr()   returns String;

excel.cds​

 

excel.js

 

 this.on('uploadLogFile', async (req) => {

        const { uploadLogFile } = require('./api/excel_v2/generalFunctionsExcelPr');


        let result;
        let file;
        let parameters = {};

        file = req.data
        parameters.FILENAME = req._queryOptions.FILENAME
        parameters.USER = req._queryOptions.USER
        parameters.DATE = req._queryOptions.UPLOAD_DATE
        parameters.TYPE = req._queryOptions.TYPE


        result = await uploadLogFile(file, parameters)

        return result

    })

 

error :

 

{
    "error": {
        "code": "415",
        "message": {
            "lang": "en",
            "value": "Unsupported Media Type"
        },
        "severity": "error",
        "target": "/#TRANSIENT#",
        "innererror": {
            "errordetails": [
                {
                    "code": "415",
                    "message": {
                        "lang": "en",
                        "value": "Unsupported Media Type"
                    },
                    "severity": "error",
                    "target": "/#TRANSIENT#"
                }
            ]
        }
    }
}

 

i am getting file from UI5 in formdata, as binary but CAP is throwing 415 error (unsupported mediatype) how to fix this issues @gregorw @SAPSupport

I also used Multer but it didnt worked for me

entry.title: The invoice amount field in the revenue schedule not automatically reflecting the invoice values
entry.content:
The invoice amount field in the revenue schedule in Manage Revenue accounting application should automatically reflect the invoice values after the creation of the billing document, but this is not happening. 

In our scenario, after billing, the invoice is not reflecting in the invoice amount field in the revenue schedule, But If we manually process  Z_FARRIC_REPOST_ADVANCED from SE38 for both sales order and its related invoices, then we run the FARR_RAI_MON for both sales orders and invoices. The invoice amount column will update in the revenue schedule in the revenue accounting app.

We need to update the invoice amount field value automatically without running this manual process.

How to solve this issue?

Dear All,

I am willing to write SAP QM C_TS414_2021 exam, I need study material.

kindly help.

Shyam

entry.title: Re: Query to create draft for a child entity in composition

entry.title: Re: Build Queries using CDS Analytical Projection Views in Eclipse
entry.content:
Thanks Mark and Giselle for a very knowledgeable blog . 

I have a general question regarding how @Consumption.filter(say Posting date: 01.09.2024 -30.09.204 is the filter value) works? Does the filter value gets passed to the table level to filter data or will it retrieve all data from the table and then filter the records at the query level CDS view based on the @Consumption.filter value? If it just filters data the Analytic Query level CDS view, Could you please help on  feasible solution for passing Consumption filter values to lower level views in embedded analytics query design?

Thanks in advance!

Regards

Mani

entry.title: Re: Friends helping friends solve the SAP Crossword puzzle
entry.content:
Thanks a lot for the new challenge!
This time was much easier than last year, but still just as interesting!

entry.title: Re: Integration of Data Action and Multi Action into File Repository (Part II)
entry.content:
Hi @JefB ,

Generally, only the "Data Actions" folder is shared with the mentioned team. But you can also try to get an overview to the shared objects from the system overview.

Got to system > overview, and click on "Authorization" tab.
Select the desired team from the team list, then click on "Fact Sheet".
A new story will be created with info to the team. Switch in this story to the tab "security" to see the list of objects shared with this team.

Cheers,
Chen Nee

entry.title: Unable to release the service order showing an error message
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am assigning to repaired order to service order system showing an error message below.

I created a service notification and created a repaired order.

Reururn delivery created,

When creating the service order via the repaired order system, it shows an error message.System not to allow release the service order.

The item category N determined by the System is not a stock item category

Message No. IW822

Regards

HN

entry.title: How can I find the status of a running iFlow by an external client e.g. a Powershell script
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I am trying to trigger an iFlow with an HTTP endpoint from a Powershell script. However it is a long running flow and I would like to have an option to monitor that from the script itself periodically. 

is there a monitoring API available that can do that? 

Thanks,

Prabal

entry.title: Re: SAP Design Studio Custom Community components aren't working after BWQ refresh??
entry.content: Anyone experience this issue in their DEV, Test or QA environments but it works in their production environments even though there hasn't been changes?

entry.title: Re: Create CAP CDS entity via synonyms
entry.content: how come my answer is truncated? you should check https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/advanced/hana

entry.title: Re: User-defined fields order
entry.content:
In SAP Business One 10.0, you can specify the display order of user-defined fields (UDFs) on a form by following these 6 steps:

1. Access the Form: Open the specific document or object where your user-defined fields are located.

2. Switch to Layout Mode: Click on "Form Settings" or the "Set Layout" option depending on the module you're in. This is usually found in the menu options. You'll be taken to a layout editor where you can modify the arrangement of fields.

3. Edit User-Defined Fields: Look for the UDFs you've created; they typically appear in the layout editor. Organize the fields by dragging them to the desired positions. The order in which you place them will determine the display order for all users.

4. Save Layout: After arranging the fields as desired, make sure to save the layout changes.

5. Adjust Form Properties: If needed, check the properties of the form to ensure the changes are set to display for all users. You can also ensure that any visibility or permission settings for the UDFs are configured correctly.

6. Exit the layout aditor and verify that the arrangement appears as intendend when you open the form.

entry.content:
Hi, experts.
Why mine TMCNV-MASKE field doesn't filled correctly, it's empty.

entry.title: Re: G/L account 2298841 where the balance of $2,811.63 still remains open
entry.content:
Hi Sridhar, please share more details.

what type of GLis this

why is there a balance

did you checked the entries via FBL3N?

whether this GL is open item managed?

entry.title: Re: Valid From date is not being considered in CS11 but C...
entry.content:
Let's see the below scenario for a better understanding,

FG Code - FG_FAN_MRP (Only one Alternate BOM)

SFG Code - SFG_FAN_MRP (have 3 Alternative BOM)

Alternative-1 Valid from 01.09.2024 assigned to Production Version 1 as PV01

Alternative-2 Valid from 02.09.2024 assigned to Production Version 2 as PV02

Alternative-3 Valid from 03.09.2024 assigned to Production Version 3 as PV03

In the MRP run and in CS11, the system is considering the Production Version-1 even though the latest valid form date is with Alternative BOM-3.

In the Costing run, the system is properly considering the alternative BOM-3 as this has the latest valid from date.

entry.content: Hi kylesladden,

entry.title: Re: CCM - Settlement date after contract end date

entry.title: Re: Groovy or XSLT to remove XML Tag(not values) in sap c...
entry.content: Thanks a lot Nagesh for your help. Marking the solution as accepted.

entry.title: Re: Splitting Ordinary Time into three Valuations
entry.content: @Naveen: Please have a look into the new capabilities of business rules in time valuation. I am pretty sure that it can cover the requirement with combination of the route time valuation type. Either you use the route function or the decision rule to find out if a time type group is above or below 30. And with the calcualation business rule you can use the modulo functionality and divide by 15...you need maybe a bit creativity and play around, but as mentioned, think this can be covered. Check my last release blog where I described the business rules for time valuation. The new one will be released in a couple of days where I wrote again a bit on it, cause we gonna make it to general availability with the H2 release.

entry.title: Navigating the Complexities of Modern Data Integration
entry.content:
Navigating the Complexities of Modern Data Integration

In today's digital landscape, organizations face a multitude of challenges due to distributed data assets, integration hurdles, and data sprawl. As data integration becomes increasingly complex, it is essential to understand the key capabilities required to effectively manage this task. This blog post highlights important data integration use cases and provides an overview of the solutions offered by SAP to help businesses streamline their data management efforts.

Key Use Cases in Data Integration

  1. Data Replication: This involves the integration of data through various methodologies such as extract, transform, load (ETL) and extract, load, transform (ELT). A common scenario is replicating data from legacy systems into SAP HANA for analytics and migration.

  2. Data Virtualization: This approach allows the creation of a virtual data layer by federating queries across remote data sources without the need for physical data movement. An example is accessing shared master data across multiple sources for seamless virtual access.

  3. Data Quality Management (DQM): DQM focuses on integrating data from different sources for cleansing, matching, and consolidation. This can include address data cleansing or embedding validation services within applications to ensure data quality.

  4. Data Orchestration: This entails pipeline-driven integration of disparate data types, supporting distributed processing. It plays a vital role in enabling data science and machine learning initiatives by integrating data from various enterprise assets.

Solutions for Enterprise Information and Data Management

SAP provides a comprehensive suite of solutions that simplify integration, allowing organizations to derive maximum value from their data landscapes while minimizing effort. These solutions encompass capabilities essential for sophisticated data operations, including data integration, metadata management, and data processing.

Data Integration: Achieving high-quality data access and movement is critical for effective system performance. While ETL methodologies have historically been the norm, SAP offers a broad range of connectivity options to accommodate diverse on-premise, cloud, and hybrid systems. This includes various adapters and connectors designed to facilitate deep integration with SAP’s business software.

Metadata Management: Organizations generate vast amounts of data in different formats, necessitating a robust understanding of this information. SAP’s metadata management capabilities enable users to catalog data effectively and assess its quality. This centralization helps identify data improvement needs, ensuring that only trustworthy data is utilized for business operations.

Data Processing: With ever-increasing data volumes, point-to-point replication is often insufficient. SAP's data processing capabilities allow for scalable solutions that meet workload demands. This includes remote orchestration and the ability to push processing to where data resides, optimizing performance and resource usage.

SAP Products and Solutions

SAP's portfolio includes various products designed to meet diverse data management needs:

  • SAP Landscape Transformation Replication Server: Facilitates real-time data replication from ABAP and non-ABAP systems into SAP HANA and other environments.

  • SAP Information Steward: Provides a unified platform for data quality monitoring and improvement, catering to data stewards and IT professionals.

  • SAP HANA Smart Data Integration: Offers real-time data replication and transformation capabilities, alongside smart data access for remote data usage without physical copying.

  • SAP Data Services: Empowers users to integrate and transform data efficiently, featuring a wide array of connectors for third-party applications.

  • SAP Data Intelligence: A comprehensive solution for managing data in multi-cloud and hybrid environments, enabling governance, integration, processing, and orchestration of data across various landscapes.

These products are specifically optimized for targeted use cases, delivering robust services tailored to individual organizational needs.

Conclusion

As organizations continue to navigate the complexities of modern data landscapes, leveraging the right tools and solutions is paramount for effective data integration. SAP's comprehensive offerings enable businesses to streamline their data management processes, ensuring they can meet evolving business requirements and drive meaningful insights from their data assets.

entry.title: Event message sending not permitted: Event type evaluation skipped
entry.content: I'm facing with a strange message, Can anyone guide me pls?
